jokes.text: Monsters!
jokes.setup: Context about steroids making players look like monsters
jokes.punchline: Simple repetitive exclamation building intensity
jokes.explanation: Basic escalation through repetition, but lacks cleverness or character specificity

jokes.text: Well. At least the Cubs will finally win.
jokes.setup: The universe could collapse from particle accelerator experiment
jokes.punchline: Silver lining that Cubs would win if reality ended
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons dark humor - finding the upside in apocalypse with perfect Cubs reference

jokes.text: Well done, idiots outer space! You have destroyed the whole of existence! Everything will be alright, I will just leave a little word.
jokes.punchline: Casual attitude about fixing universal destruction with 'a little word'
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between cosmic disaster and mundane solution approach

jokes.text: Hey, Lisa. I bet I can jump in the pool here. / Bart, it would be the thing most stupid t'aurais as ever. / No. The most stupid thing would be to do it over.
jokes.setup: Bart wants to do something stupid
jokes.punchline: Immediately escalating to doing it twice being even dumber
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - acknowledging stupidity while immediately planning worse stupidity

jokes.text: I am afraid that your son is in a deep coma which he never emerge. / I guess we should thank God he is not dead. / I know - that way, I continue to charge you.
jokes.setup: Doctor delivering terrible news about Bart's coma
jokes.punchline: Doctor's mercenary motivation for keeping patient alive
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of healthcare system with perfect cynical doctor character

jokes.text: A robot? A child robot would be great. We can make it confusing for his head explodes. 'This sentence is a lie' But if it's a lie, then it must be true. But if that is true, then it must be ... Whoop! Whoop! Whoop! Kaboom!
jokes.punchline: Classic logical paradox causing robot malfunction
jokes.explanation: Classic sci-fi trope executed well with good buildup and payoff sound effects

jokes.text: And a robot you would forget your son died very quickly. / I thought it was in a coma! / It is almost the same thing. Except that like that, I continue to send you bills. / You've already said that. / Well, you did not laugh the first time.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment that the joke didn't land
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about failed comedy - risky but works as character moment

jokes.text: Mom, it hurts! / He knows how to operate all my buttons. / And voila how activate his. / I am a man who loves to strangle her children. Do you think I can ... / Of course, try. / Kind of metallic ... Wow! The trachea is incredibly responsive.
jokes.punchline: Homer testing strangling capabilities with professional assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - treating child strangulation as technical feature to evaluate

jokes.text: Yeah, that's the oh-sixes for you.
jokes.punchline: Playing on '06 model year like it's a car
jokes.explanation: Decent throwaway line treating robots like consumer products

jokes.text: No bot is not a robot! My Flanderinouilles! Having a brother robot me. The implications ethnic make me tense.
jokes.punchline: Making up 'Flanderinouilles' exclamation and worried about robot ethics
jokes.explanation: Good Flanders characterization with made-up exclamation and overthinking robot rights

jokes.text: You replaced me with this toy? / Replace is an ugly word. There you updated. / Bart, we can be friends. / Will eat my shorts ... / I will t'obéir.
jokes.punchline: Robot's literal compliance with 'eat my shorts' command
jokes.explanation: Great payoff on Bart's catchphrase - robot takes literal command seriously

jokes.text: In nature, they would never experienced boredom, obesity, the loss of a purpose ... You know, the American dream.
jokes.setup: Homer defending zoos as giving animals new experiences
jokes.punchline: Equating negative experiences with American dream
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark social commentary disguised as Homer's typical misunderstanding

jokes.text: God thank you, I landed in a bird pens.
jokes.punchline: Relief at landing in presumably soft bird enclosure
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup with predictable relief punchline

jokes.text: There should be a law that punishes the abandonment of children. / Do not be afraid of us. We're kind of robots were rejected, like you. / I'm not a robot, I'm human. / Tell me, young man. How does it feel to have ... feelings? / I say that I was a human, not a daughter.
jokes.punchline: Bart's confusion about being called daughter instead of addressing feelings
jokes.explanation: Weak misdirection that doesn't land cleanly - translation issues may be involved

jokes.text: The name of a command prompt! Our plays have disappeared! We were robbed! My pieces are gone. This kid screwed us all. It pissed me over! You're a robot-urinal. Lava-tron.
jokes.punchline: Robot insults culminating in 'robot-urinal' and 'Lava-tron'
jokes.explanation: Creative robot-specific insults with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: It stinks! I have a small robot legs. and a culture that is not equipped for an adult.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently has robot parts attached
jokes.punchline: Complaining about impractical robot leg size and cultural mismatch
jokes.explanation: Moderate visual gag about mismatched robot parts

jokes.text: I will call for their job that we may not come. / Freak.
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt one-word dismissal
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Homer rudeness with good economy

jokes.text: Honey, animals do not feel the death. It has been proven by scientists Buffalo Grill.
jokes.setup: Lisa's concern about hunting being cruel
jokes.punchline: Homer citing restaurant chain as scientific authority
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of confirmation bias - using clearly biased source as scientific authority

jokes.text: Stop your hay - Dad will kill wild animals but I shot a bird and I gotta go see a psychiatrist. / He still believes that Hobo was a bird.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about double standard on killing
jokes.punchline: Revealing Bart actually killed a homeless person, not a bird
jokes.explanation: Shocking dark humor reveal with perfect casual delivery - classic Simpsons boundary-pushing

jokes.text: Oh my god, I see coming: He wants to sell his mansion in timeshare. / Well, I already have credits, then t'ait is you who have done!
jokes.setup: Trying to guess Burns' sinister plot
jokes.punchline: Mundane timeshare sales pitch with domestic blame-shifting
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection from sinister to mundane but execution is clunky

jokes.text: Five minutes of racing? Shoot me now. / Well, it broke the ice.
jokes.setup: Homer's lazy response to death hunt challenge
jokes.punchline: Burns treating shooting as social ice-breaker
jokes.explanation: Good character moments - Homer's laziness meeting Burns' casual evil

jokes.text: Well, I guess you like hunting, and you said in the past, killing people is part of my religion ... I think I can do something with it.
jokes.setup: Lawyer asked to justify Burns hunting humans
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing Burns' homicidal religious beliefs as legal strategy
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about finding legal loopholes for murder via religious freedom

jokes.text: You can not kill a Hindu! / Help me, Jesus!
jokes.setup: Apu claiming religious protection from death
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning Hinduism for Christianity when threatened
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about religious convenience under pressure

jokes.text: Well, we used to say good Race always beat a good hunt. But the stats say, 'bet on the guy with the gun!'
jokes.setup: Sports cliché about racing versus hunting
jokes.punchline: Obvious statistical advantage of armed person
jokes.explanation: Decent sports commentary parody with obvious but effective punchline

jokes.text: 'Jackpot, a million dollars!' If I could get out of here alive, I could ...
jokes.setup: Homer facing death finds winning lottery ticket
jokes.punchline: Irony of winning money when about to die
jokes.explanation: Standard situational irony - good setup but predictable payoff

jokes.text: Homer, Burns we only hunting for six hours and you already resorted to cannibalism. / And there are bananas in that tree up there. / Ho, them seem a little green.
jokes.punchline: Homer's pickiness about fruit ripeness while engaging in cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast between extreme and mundane pickiness - perfect Homer character logic

jokes.text: Fooled with the body of my best friend.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently using Lenny's corpse as disguise
jokes.punchline: Casual attitude toward desecrating friend's body for survival
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about friendship and survival with Homer's typical moral flexibility

jokes.text: Stay out over 18 hours and not a single call. / Oh, I can not stay angry against you when the pot feels the sausage and onion.
jokes.punchline: Immediately forgiven due to cooking smells
jokes.explanation: Good Marge characterization - practical priorities over emotional complaints

jokes.text: I spoke too soon? Go! It will live in my playbook!
jokes.punchline: Excited about adding new experience to sports knowledge
jokes.explanation: Decent sports commentator parody with professional enthusiasm for awkwardness

jokes.text: Look, Grand Father disguised as a gorilla. Bob Tahiti in Spiderman, and Nelson is a raccoon. / I'm not a raccoon, I am a lone Ranger. But I am too poor to pay me the hat. It is not even a mask. It's fat turnover.
jokes.punchline: Nelson's poverty forcing creative costume substitutions
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about economic constraints on Halloween creativity

jokes.text: Because I am a black Dracula, I must Blackula be? / My wife told me not to come to Dracula. But I replied, 'Bernice, we are in the 21st century ...'
jokes.punchline: Questioning racial assumptions in costume naming with progressive marriage dynamic
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary through character costume choice with good relationship humor

jokes.text: Jester. With $ 25, can not even have half a cereal bar. I exaggerate, but more seriously, my prices are very high.
jokes.setup: Apu commenting on contest prize value
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of price gouging with false modesty
jokes.explanation: Good Apu characterization - honest about being dishonest with customers

jokes.text: I do not costume. I am a real witch. / Wait a bit, it's cheating! Burn ... and make the purchase order!
jokes.punchline: Still demanding contest prize despite technically cheating
jokes.explanation: Decent supernatural twist with consumer entitlement humor

jokes.text: This is not fair. I do not even wear no disguise. Look at me, I am a young gorilla virvoltant
jokes.punchline: He's turned into a 'virvoltant' (spinning?) gorilla despite no costume
jokes.explanation: Good Grandpa moment complaining about unfairness while describing absurd transformation

jokes.text: Look at me, I am a young gorilla virvoltant. My hip is broken. With the help. Let's go to the hospital for a gorilla.
jokes.punchline: Still having old man health problems despite young animal body
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - transformation doesn't fix Grampa's fundamental health issues

jokes.text: Listen to me, I am Oberon, Queen of the Fairies. / You're a big cockroach, and you will die!
jokes.setup: Homer grandly declaring fairy royalty status
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatened with death for being disgusting insect
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Homer's grandiose self-image with harsh reality

jokes.text: Maggie is now a real witch! It can cancel this spell. / I do not want to cancel this fate. I can howl at the moon and eat rats.
jokes.punchline: Bart enjoying his new animal abilities
jokes.explanation: Good Bart characterization - preferring wild animal freedom over human responsibility

jokes.text: I do not want change either. I can get horses mud. Girls will love! And I'm Jared from the subway ads. I have just a little overweight, and ambiguous sexuality.
jokes.setup: Characters not wanting to change back from transformations
jokes.punchline: Martin's oddly specific and uncomfortable Subway spokesman identification
jokes.explanation: Weird and specific pop culture reference with uncomfortable implications - classic Simpsons oddness

jokes.text: Very good. This concludes the show Halloween this year. I hope you take as much fun to watch us Koreen that have been conducting animation. But there are some people for whom it is Halloween every day. I speak of illiterate adults. For them, try to read the newspaper, it's terrifying than any of the goblins, Goule, ghosts or spirits. So please, make donations to 'Children's book. Together, we can their to read a slam dunk. / Dennis Rodman? What are you doing here? / Community service after a speeding. Happy Halloween to all!
jokes.punchline: Dennis Rodman appearing for community service obligation
jokes.explanation: Good meta ending with celebrity cameo twist - typical Simpsons deflation of earnest messaging
